frameworks.name: sequential sampling / evidence integration
frameworks.one_line: decision formed by accumulating samples of evidence until commitment

frameworks.name: short-term memory retrieval
frameworks.one_line: evidence samples retrieved from memory when stimulus is gone

frameworks.name: embodied / motor-yoked decision-making
frameworks.one_line: standard view where evidence integration occurs in action-selection circuits

core_question: How do monkeys form perceptual decisions when the motor actions required to report the choice are not yet known?
key_assumptions: LIP activity reflects evidence accumulation toward a choice
decision can be temporally decoupled from motor planning
samples of evidence are retainable in short-term memory
manipulations: targets revealed at unpredictable locations after motion stimulus offset
abstract color-direction association vs direct action mapping
delay between motion offset and target onset
key_findings: monkeys postponed decision formation until targets appeared
LIP neurons represented evidence integration after stimulus offset
evidence samples must be retrieved from short-term memory when stimulus is no longer visible
interpretations: decision formation is protracted until the choice can be framed in motor terms
abstract decisions recruit memory-based sampling rather than concurrent motor-yoked integration
